resolution: options-list's 11 rows appear in the SAME §4.5-verbatim order (init.defaultBranch -> core.ignorecase -> core.autocrlf/eol -> user.email (global) -> push.autoSetupRemote -> pull.rebase -> fetch.prune -> alias -> color -> merge.conflictstyle -> diff.colorMoved) in both media; option-detail's current/recommended-then-explanation-then-advisory order matches; fix-preview's banner-then-diff-then-managed-block-note order matches; confirm-write's nothing-changed-banner-then-sentinel-text order matches; backup-notice's path-then-explainer order matches; result-applied's success-message-then-user.email-restated-then-backup-path order matches. Verified via html/*.png and tui/*.png side-by-side review (FIELDS.md) and TestGlobalGit_EveryScreenRendersNonEmptyWithSignatureAndBreadcrumb.

Labels and helper copy match verbatim between media wherever both are present: the advisory banner text, init.defaultBranch's full multi-paragraph option-detail explanation, the managed-block containment note, the backup path and explainer, and the result-applied success/user.email-restated/restore copy are all byte-identical (recipeFixtures.ts's globalGit* exports vs surface_globalgit.go's ggit* Go constants). ONE accepted exception: options-list's per-option one-liner explanation is TUI-omitted (viewport compaction, CRITIQUE.md finding #2) while present in HTML, and fix-preview/confirm-write's TUI rendering GROUPS multiple key=value pairs onto shared compact lines (ggitCompactValueLines) rather than the full per-section HTML block — logged in FIELDS.md and CRITIQUE.md finding #2, not a labels-and-helper-copy-verbatim wording mismatch because every value present in the compact form is byte-identical to its HTML counterpart, only the grouping/layout differs (a live-PTY layout constraint, not a wording mismatch).

The GGIT-01 11-option baseline + recipe-defaults set (init.defaultBranch, core.ignorecase, core.autocrlf/eol, user.email (global), push.autoSetupRemote, pull.rebase, fetch.prune, alias (8 shortcuts), color (ui/branch/diff/status), merge.conflictstyle, diff.colorMoved) appears in the same order with the same current/recommended values in both media (options-list.png html/tui pair; TestGlobalGit_BaselineOptionSetAnchors). The needsAction split (10 recommended, 1 informational) and the resulting glyph assignment (!/✓) match exactly, including the 8 aliases (st, co, br, ci, df, lg, unstage, last) and the 4 color settings (ui, branch, diff, status = auto).

init.defaultBranch=main, core.ignorecase=false, push.autoSetupRemote=true, pull.rebase=true, fetch.prune=true, merge.conflictstyle=diff3, diff.colorMoved=zebra are interpolated DIRECTLY from the shared, unmodified globalGitDefaults fixture (02-01/02-02) in the HTML mockup, and mirrored as byte-identical Go string literals in surface_globalgit.go -- a single source of truth, never duplicated with drift. core.autocrlf=input/core.eol=lf, the 8 aliases, and the 4 color=auto settings match recipes/gitconfig.recipe's own commented ~/.gitconfig_default example block verbatim (# autocrlf = input; # st = status / co = checkout / br = branch / ci = commit / df = diff / lg = log --graph ... / unstage = reset HEAD -- / last = log -1 HEAD; # color ui/branch/diff/status = auto). Global user.email is correctly modeled as NOT written by gitid (D-04b: 'No user section is ever emitted'), matching the real internal/gitconfig/baseline.go RenderBaselineBlock's own documented behavior -- the mockup does not invent a write gitid's real backend never performs.

All 6 named states from UX-DIRECTION.md §4.5 exist as distinct, navigable screens in both media (manifest.json screen list == surface_globalgit.go ScreenDef IDs == route file titles), reachable via the SAME transitions in both: options-list -> option-detail (v) -> fix-preview (f) -> confirm-write (w) -> backup-notice (y) -> result-applied (z). Verified by TestGlobalGit_KeysFormAConnectedPathReachingEveryScreen (Go), manual html/tui screenshot review, and the surface-scoped dummy-nav e2e (TestDummyNavReachesAllScreens/global-git) reaching every screen through the running binary -- all 6 subtests pass, including fix-preview/confirm-write after the CRITIQUE.md finding #2 compaction fix.

The mutation ceremony's all 4 beats (§5) appear on the same steps in both media: fix-preview is beat 1 (read-only diff preview), confirm-write is beats 1+2 (exact sentinel-visible text + explicit confirm keystroke), backup-notice is beat 3 (timestamped ~/.gitconfig backup path), result-applied is beat 4 (what changed + how to restore). Managed-block containment is shown, not just asserted: confirm-write renders the # BEGIN/END gitid managed: global-git sentinels around the managed block content (HTML: full text; TUI: the compacted-but-complete field set), identical field values in both media. Verified via fix-preview.png/confirm-write.png/backup-notice.png/result-applied.png html/tui pairs and TestGlobalGit_ManagedBlockContainmentShown.

Every intra-flow key (v/f/w/y/z) is shown in the keybar/footer of the screen where it applies in BOTH media (Keybar.tsx entries vs renderShellKeybar output); the global-git keys allocate v=option-detail/f=fix-preview/w=confirm-write/y=backup-notice/z=result-applied from the SAME linear chain global-ssh's own key allocation uses (never a collision -- each surface's ScreenDef.Keys is scoped to itself, and only one primary surface is active at a time); never n/g, create-flow's/git-screen's own LaunchKeys (TestGlobalGit_IntraSurfaceKeysNeverReuseLaunchKeysNOrG); reserved keys (Esc/q/?//) are consistent across every screenshot pair reviewed. See CRITIQUE.md finding #3 for the one accepted label-WORDING difference (TUI shows the raw target screen ID, HTML shows a semantic action phrase -- the key itself and its destination match), the same class already accepted for git-screen/identity-manager/global-ssh.

GGIT-01's own dedicated highlight for init.defaultBranch (the option carrying the highest onboarding visibility per §4.5). HTML renders an outlined amber Chip labeled "main vs master" on both options-list and option-detail; TUI renders a trailing "[main vs master]" bracket on the same compact line on both screens. Both media name BOTH branch names ("main" and "master", never just one), and both media's option-detail full explanation states Git's own compiled-in default is still "master", that init.defaultBranch=main only affects newly-created repositories, and that existing "master" branches are never renamed by this setting. Verified via options-list.png/option-detail.png html/tui pairs and TestGlobalGit_MainVsMasterHighlighted (asserts "main vs master", "main", and "master" all present on both screens in the live TUI render).

GGIT-01's highest-risk affordance (§4.5, §5): writes must preserve content outside the managed block verbatim. fix-preview and confirm-write both state, byte-identically between media, "gitid only owns the block between its sentinels -- everything else in ~/.gitconfig ... is preserved verbatim"; confirm-write renders the literal # BEGIN gitid managed: global-git / # END gitid managed: global-git sentinel lines around the managed block content in BOTH media (HTML: the full multi-section text via globalGitFullManagedBlockText; TUI: the field-complete compacted form via ggitCompactValueLines, same values, grouped for the 80x24 viewport per CRITIQUE.md finding #2); result-applied restates "Everything outside the sentinels -- including any hand-written [user]/[includeIf]/[url] sections -- was preserved verbatim", naming concrete examples. Global user.email's absence from the managed block (a structural gitid rule, D-04b -- not a user decline) is stated explicitly on every ceremony screen (fix-preview, confirm-write, result-applied) in both media, never silently dropped. Verified via confirm-write.png/fix-preview.png/result-applied.png html/tui pairs, TestGlobalGit_ManagedBlockContainmentShown, and TestGlobalGit_AdvisoryNeverBlocking's user.email-stays-visible check.
